summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures a delicately paced, Shakedown Street‑filled show with standout Minglewood, China Doll and Space performances.
review: The AUD source is remarkably clean – almost matrix quality – with pinpoint instrument separation despite occasional vocal roughness and a whistling intrusion early in the set. Musically the band rolls at a slower, more lyrical tempo, delivering hot versions of Shakedown Street, Supplication, Minglewood and a soaring China Doll, while the second‑set jazzy explorations (Space, Lazy Lightning > Supp) are praised as among the best of the era.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating is ~4.1; most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and highlight superb sound plus strong performances, outweighing a few isolated negative comments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear, matrix‑like separation; vocals occasionally rough; early‑set whistling noise noted but overall superb clarity and balance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A slower, more delicate tempo gives the concert a smooth, lyrical feel; first set is relaxed but engaging, while the second set moves into jazzy, tightly‑played explorations that many reviewers cite as highlights.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for expressive, searching guitar work and emotive vocal delivery (especially on Althea); highlighted as a key driver of the show’s magic.
band_member_comments.Phil: Solid bass foundation noted; traditional lines early with more exploratory playing later in the second set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Front‑and‑center in the mix; slide work on Minglewood praised, overall strong contribution to the hot moments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent-era keyboards described as pleasantly psychedelic (e.g., on Cassidy) and integral to the atmospheric Space segment.
